report_name: Critical 'DifyTap' Flaws in Dify AI Platform Expose Cross-Tenant Data in 1M+ Apps

tools.name: Dify
tools.description: An open-source AI application development platform affected by the DifyTap vulnerabilities, which could enable cross-tenant data access and internal API exposure.

tools.name: PDFium
tools.description: A PDF rendering library used by Dify. An outdated version vulnerable to CVE-2024-5846 was in use for over 18 months, potentially allowing for code execution.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41947
vulnerabilities.description: A critical flaw in Dify that allows a user to attach a file belonging to another user within the same tenant to their own chat session, leading to unauthorized access to sensitive documents.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41948
vulnerabilities.description: A critical, unauthenticated vulnerability in Dify that could allow an attacker to access internal Plugin Daemon endpoints, potentially leading to further compromise of the Dify infrastructure.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41949
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Dify that allowed a user to preview documents uploaded by other tenants, breaking data segregation between different customers on the cloud platform.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41950
vulnerabilities.description: An Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) logic flaw in Dify where the platform only validated a user's tenant ID when linking a file, but not when accessing it, allowing cross-tenant file access with a guessed file ID.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2024-5846
vulnerabilities.description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the PDFium library used by Dify, which could allow for code execution by uploading a malicious PDF file.

identities.name: Volvo
identities.description: A major enterprise mentioned as a user of the Dify platform, potentially at risk from the DifyTap vulnerabilities.

identities.name: Panasonic
identities.description: A major enterprise mentioned as a user of the Dify platform, potentially at risk from the DifyTap vulnerabilities.

infrastructure.name: Dify Plugin Daemon
infrastructure.description: Internal Dify service endpoints that were exposed to unauthenticated access due to vulnerability CVE-2026-41948.

indicators.name: Suspicious Dify File Preview Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Dify File Preview Access
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a5b-8c6d-7e8f9a0b1c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to the Dify file preview endpoint, which could be abused via CVE-2026-41949 to view documents from other tenants.
references:
  - https://securitybrief.com.au/story/dify-flaws-expose-cross-tenant-ai-data-zafran-says
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1530
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-uri-stem|startswith: '/files/preview/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests to the Dify file preview endpoint. This endpoint was associated with CVE-2026-41949, which allowed users to preview documents from other tenants.
